steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage says rising prices had a "counterintuitive effect" on demand: buyers who hadn't previously wanted the dolls suddenly flooded the market. The blank must describe what the price increase did to demand — it produced or brought about demand that wasn't there before.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'satisfying': satisfying demand means fulfilling it, implying demand already existed. The passage says buyers "hadn't previously wanted" the dolls, so demand was created, not fulfilled. ✗
  • Choice B — 'exploiting': exploiting demand means taking advantage of demand that already exists. Again, the passage shows demand was newly generated. ✗
  • Choice C — 'capitalizing': capitalizing on demand means profiting from existing demand. Same problem — no pre-existing demand to capitalize on. ✗
  • Choice D — 'eliciting': eliciting means drawing out or bringing about. The price rise elicited (called into existence) demand that wasn't previously there — exactly matching the counterintuitive scenario described. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
